rationale: The cited paper is not merely listed in a background cluster; it is used at a specific interpretive point in the General Discussion. The citing authors reference Wang and Lee (2006) to support the idea that regulatory compatibility may work through selective attention and heuristic processing rather than extensive deliberation. Although this citation does not drive the paper's hypotheses or empirical design, it meaningfully informs how the authors explain their findings.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Wang and Lee (2006) as process-level support when interpreting its regulatory compatibility effects, arguing that the effects may reflect selective attention/heuristic processing. This is a conceptual interpretation link rather than a direct methodological or hypothesis-building dependency.
